pests.name: Fall Armyworm (FAW)
pests.identification: Look for egg masses on leaves (100–200 eggs), caterpillars with inverted Y on head capsule

cultural: Early sowing to avoid peak infestation
management.note: Apply spray directly into whorl. Add sand to improve coverage.

pests.name: Bollworm Complex
pests.scientific: Helicoverpa armigera, Pectinophora gossypiella, Earias spp.
symptoms: Entry and exit holes in bolls

cultural: Use Bt cotton for H. armigera

pests.name: Whitefly
symptoms: CLCuD (Cotton Leaf Curl Disease) in cotton

diseases.name: Wheat Rust (Yellow/Brown/Black)
diseases.pathogen: Puccinia striiformis, P. triticina, P. graminis

symptoms.yellow_rust: Yellow powdery stripes along leaf veins
symptoms.black_rust: Dark brown-black elongated pustules on stems and leaves

diseases.name: Rice Blast
symptoms: Diamond/spindle-shaped grey lesions with brown borders on leaves (leaf blast)
Dark green-grey water-soaked areas on neck (neck blast)
Rotten, empty or partially filled grains

diseases.name: Late Blight (Potato/Tomato)
symptoms: Water-soaked dark lesions on leaves expanding rapidly
White fungal growth on lower surface
Fast crop collapse in humid weather

diseases.name: Bacterial Leaf Blight (BLB)
symptoms: Water-soaked lesions on leaf margins turning yellow then white
Kresek phase – wilting and death of young seedlings

cultural: Avoid excess N in flood-prone areas
chemical: Streptomycin sulphate + Tetracycline 1 g/litre (combined with copper)

ipm_principles.description: Integrated Pest Management (IPM) combines cultural, biological, physical, and chemical methods to manage pests economically and ecologically.
steps: 1. Monitor fields regularly (twice weekly during critical periods)
2. Identify pests correctly before taking action
3. Check economic threshold levels (ETL) before spraying
4. Use cultural methods first (rotation, resistant varieties, crop sanitation)
5. Encourage beneficial insects (lady beetles, parasitic wasps)
6. Use biological pesticides (Bt, HaNPV, Trichogramma) before synthetic chemicals
7. If chemicals needed, choose selective and least toxic options
8. Rotate chemical groups to prevent resistance
ipm_principles.benefits: IPM reduces input costs by 30–40%, protects environment, preserves natural enemies, and produces safer food.
